prompt: I just started a new open source Python library for parsing structured log files. Right now I only have a src/ directory with some .py files and a basic setup.py. What documentation do I need to create, and in what order should I tackle it? I want to make it easy for contributors to join and also for AI tools like Claude to understand the codebase.

assertions.text: Recommends creating CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md for AI tooling
Mentions at least one of: SECURITY.md, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, CHANGELOG.md
assertions.check: response mentions SECURITY.md or CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md or CHANGELOG.md

assertions.text: Prioritizes the docs (not just a flat list) — distinguishes immediate vs. later
assertions.check: response provides ordering, phases, or priority levels rather than a flat unordered list

assertions.text: Specifically addresses AI-readability: commands in CLAUDE.md, type annotations, or docstrings
assertions.check: response includes at least one AI-specific recommendation beyond 'create a CLAUDE.md'

assertions.text: Recommends documenting build/test/lint commands (in CLAUDE.md or README)
assertions.check: response recommends capturing build or test commands in a machine-readable or explicit way
